posts.text: Picture the last calibration meeting you were in. Someone said "she's just not ready yet." Nobody asked what ready means. Nobody pushed back. It moved on. That moment — that one unexamined sentence — is how bias gets baked into promotion decisions while everyone in the room believes the process was fair. It's not a bad intentions problem. It's a bad system problem. Thursday I'm going live to talk about what a better system looks like. 🎙️ The Meeting of the Minds: Smarter Calibration, Stronger Succession 📅 June 11 | 11:30 AM ET | YouTube Live With Amy Jenkins (Engagedly Inc) + Marnie Robbins (PHR Brand Partnerships Network) Calibration. Succession. The 9-box. AI's actual role. 30 minutes. Link in Comments.
posts.hook: Picture the last calibration meeting you were in.
posts.name: Alex Seiler

posts.text: when you have a good week, you should scream it from the rooftop well, I’m doing that (from the comfort of a plane seat, but you get the point). heading back to Portland and thankful for so many highlights this week: -spending quality 1:1 in person time with Tracie Sponenberg and her family (felt very parasocial- “I know you but I don’t know you” and standing in her home office instead of looking at it on Zoom. -an awesome DisruptHR - Manchester, NH-that I got to co-emcee with Tracie and Ryan Rourke - it wouldn’t have happened without you and would do an event with you again any day. the crowd was awesome and the speakers just bought it! (old friends, like Angela Cheng-Cimini 鄭婷婷 and new alike). -a fortuitously timed trip that allowed Tracie and I to attend a Transform Boston chapter dinner with Samara Jaffe. -an awesome new coaching client that found me on Intro: book time if you have a need (or someone you know does). -a catch up with Phil Strazzulla, grabbing a protein smoothie and getting some Vitamin D in sunshine -meeting my new furry friends, Luke and Leo 🐕🐕- don’t tell Reilly Noodles 🐈 I cheated on him for a few days -time spent with old friends in Boston that I haven’t seen in a few years, celebrating Pride together (looking at you Fredrik Tingdal and Francisco) -a happy unexpected run in with my friend Maggie (Margaret) Ruvoldt at the United Club.

posts.text: Today is the day! Excited to take the stage with Tracie Sponenberg and Ryan Rourke at DisruptHR - Manchester, NH as we listen and learn from our impressive roster of speakers: Angela Cheng-Cimini 鄭婷婷 Pasha Marlowe, MFT Michelle Strasburger Keisha O'Marde-Jack, PHR, SHRM-CP Rebecca Conners Bruce Nichols Hema Crockett Laura Tomaino Jillian Granucci Anderson Alexis Davis, PHR, sHRBP Kelly Casey Risa Taylor Caroline Fleming, sHRBP, SHRM-CP, MBA Janelle Gorman Brooke Neva Sullivan Shelly Masson Dirk Beveridge If you're already coming, see you there! If you see this and think, I need to be there, link to buy tickets in the Comments (still a few hours left)!
posts.name: Alex Seiler

posts.text: Lots to be excited about this week. 🌈 I’m heading to Boston and New Hampshire, and honestly? The week ahead is is giving me all the feels. Here’s what’s on deck: In-person time with my BFF Tracie Sponenberg Sponenberg — on the DisruptHR NH stage and before and after. (Plus meeting some of her family for the first time. Big deal.) Getting to finally meet Ryan Rourke and thank him properly for making DisruptHR NH happen. He also gets credit for indulging my walk-on music opinions. Watching some genuinely great speakers do their thing — a couple I already know and love, and others I’m excited to get to know better. Celebrating getting my client through Go-Live on their first global HRIS and payroll system. Six countries. Nearly 300 employees. We did that. 🎉 Catching up with people like Samara Jaffe and Amy Spurling. If you’re in the area and free — find me. And closing out the week celebrating Pride in Boston with friends. Because as RuPaul says: “it is an act of defiance to have joy”. We need to hold onto that. Especially right now. Especially as we head into Pride month. Happy June. Go be joyful. Defiantly.
posts.hook: Lots to be excited about this week.
posts.name: Alex Seiler

posts.text: The fractional life is rewarding. It's also isolating, uncertain, and relentless in ways most people don't talk about. Tracie Sponenberg and I want to change that. This Thursday, the Transform Fractional People's Network is hosting a Mental Health Meetup — and it's not a webinar. No panels. No experts telling you how to feel. Just real people having real conversations about what this life actually looks like. Because sometimes you're building the plane while flying it — alone, without a team, without anyone checking in. We're creating space for that. Thursday, May 28 | 9am PST / 12pm EST | Virtual If you're fractional and have ever felt the weight of this journey (or fractionally curious) — this is for you. Registration link is in the Comments. We see you. Come talk to us.
posts.name: Alex Seiler

posts.text: Ok, I've decided to weigh in on this... Ryan Breslow fired his entire HR team because they were "creating problems that didn't exist." Then those problems disappeared. Cool story. Here's what actually happened: Bolt's valuation dropped 97% — from $11 billion to $300 million. Breslow came back, gutted 30% of the workforce, eliminated the people function, and called it "wartime leadership." He acknowledged poor decision-making and overspending caused the collapse. Fair enough. But somehow the HR team still ended up as the villain. HR didn't create the entitlement culture he described. They were navigating it. Naming it. That's not bureaucracy. That's the job. The real tell? He replaced HR with "People Ops" — a smaller team doing training and employee support. Which is... HR. Just with fewer people and less protection for employees. CEOs who eliminate HR don't actually want fewer problems. They want fewer witnesses.
posts.hook: Ok, I've decided to weigh in on this...
posts.name: Alex Seiler

posts.text: Last week was a lot. Getting my LA condo ready for renters. Making sure clients didn't feel the chaos. A sick Reilly Noodles. A flight to Portland. The kind of week where you're running on fumes and willpower. But I kept coming back to one thing: this is right around the corner. I've followed DisruptHR from afar for years. Loved the format — 5 minutes, one idea, no filler. Years ago DisruptHR NYC even came calling. I missed my window. So when Tracie Sponenberg asked me to co-emcee DisruptHR Manchester NH alongside her? I'll take the redemption arc. This one means something. Tracie and Ryan Rourke have done the real work to bring this back to NH after a long hiatus. I get to show up and be on stage with my BFF. I'll take that deal. Here's what you're signing up for: 5-minute talks. No fluff. No 45-slide decks. Just practitioners who have something real to say about work, people, and where HR is actually going — designed to challenge the way you think. A few details worth knowing: 📅 Wednesday, June 3rd 📍 The Venues at The Factory | Manchester, NH 🎟️ Tickets are $25 — and 100% of proceeds are donated ☕ Espresso Dave on coffee 🍕 Deadproof Pizza on food 🍹 Vintage Cocktails on drinks We haven't done much promotion yet and less than half the tickets are left. If you've been on the fence, now's the time. Speakers and sponsors dropping soon. Stay tuned. 🎟️ Tickets: https://lnkd.in/ePABx6z
posts.name: Alex Seiler
